pivot_score_rank: THIRD in BibleWorld history (model-parity 8.90, prompt-lock 8.70, semantic-pass-k 8.65)
key_patterns: PAT-062 — Numbers 23:19 (The Perfect Consistency Standard Pattern, Level 3, score 9.2/10): Balaam's second oracle encodes a three-part consistency verification protocol: declare behavioral invariant → run empirical behavioral test ('Does he speak and then not act?') → verify null discrepancy result ('Does he promise and then not fulfill?'). Consistency is NOT an architectural claim — it is a cross-run behavioral measurement. Maps to semantic-pass-k: run agent k times → embed outputs → pairwise cosine similarity → ConsistencyScore → CI gate against criticality-tier threshold.

PAT-063 — Genesis 5:1-32 (Schema-Enforced Lineage Pattern, Level 2, score 7.5/10): Uniform genealogical schema enables anomaly detection — Enoch's translation is identifiable only because every other record has a death statement. Schema consistency is a precondition for behavioral anomaly detection. Reinforces drift-guard v2 roadmap.

PAT-064 — John 2:24-25 (Internal State Transparency Pattern, Level 2, score 7.9/10): 'He knew what was in each person' — direct internal state access eliminates the need for behavioral consistency testing. Interpretability and behavioral measurement tools are in a substitution relationship. As Anthropic's interpretability research matures, behavioral tools (semantic-pass-k, cot-fidelity) become bridge technologies. Acquisition window: 3-5 years before interpretability renders behavioral measurement redundant.
pain_point_solved: AI agents are non-deterministic. The same task, run k times, produces k different outputs. arXiv 2602.16666 (Feb 2026) documents that 'consistency and robustness do not improve reliably across agents' — 14 models evaluated. τ-bench: 80% pass^1 → 25% pass^8 on identical tasks. CV scores: Claude 4.5 (15.2%), GPT-5 (32.2%), Llama (47.0%). Promptfoo GitHub issue #5947: direct feature request 'Support pass^N metric for evaluating consistency across repeated test runs' — filed, unimplemented. No pip library produces ConsistencyScore (semantic pass^k) as a named CI-gateable metric with task-criticality-tier thresholds. semantic-pass-k: run agent k times → embed all outputs with sentence-transformers → pairwise cosine similarity matrix → ConsistencyScore = mean upper triangle → compare against criticality-tier threshold (CRITICAL: 0.99, HIGH: 0.90, MEDIUM: 0.75, LOW: 0.60) → CI gate pass/fail.
competitive_moat: GREEN [WEB-FRESH 2026-03-31] — 15+ tools audited: AgentAssay (qualixar/agentassay, arXiv 2603.02601 Mar 2026 — EXISTS but different question: 'how many runs?' not 'what is the semantic score?'), EvalView (tool call snapshot/diff — different level), Judge Reliability Harness (judges, not agents), DeepEval (no pass^k CI gate), Langfuse/LangSmith (tracing, not k-run consistency), Arize Phoenix (distribution, not task consistency), MAESTRO (research paper), ReliabilityBench (research paper). NONE produce ConsistencyScore as a named CI-gateable metric with criticality tiers. Window: 3-6 months.

semantic_pass_k_summary.core_api: ConsistencyRunner + ConsistencyReport + CriticalityTier + ConsistencyBudget + @consistency_probe decorator + CLI (spk run/report/compare/gate) + pytest plugin
semantic_pass_k_summary.key_innovation: Task-criticality-tiered ConsistencyScore (semantic pass^k) as a first-class CI gate metric. Pairwise cosine similarity matrix across k embedded agent outputs → ConsistencyScore → compare against CRITICAL(0.99)/HIGH(0.90)/MEDIUM(0.75)/LOW(0.60). First tool to make 'is my agent consistent enough for this task criticality level?' a single-command answer.
semantic_pass_k_summary.implementation_status: API spec complete, sprint plan written (6-8 weeks), known unknowns documented (KU-044 through KU-047)
semantic_pass_k_summary.dependencies: sentence-transformers, click, rich, numpy, sqlite3 (stdlib), pytest (optional)

next_cycle_directions: DIRECTION 1 — BEAT model-parity (8.90 ALL-TIME RECORD): semantic-pass-k scored 8.65 — 0.25 below the record. The next BIG_TECH_GAP_ANALYSIS cycle (cycle 021 based on rotation: 020=A, 021=H) needs to find a gap more acutely painful. Candidate from PAT-064: the interpretability gap — tools that bridge Anthropic's interpretability research and production engineering. What does a developer do with mechanistic interpretability? No tooling exists. Could score 9.0+.

DIRECTION 2 — SCRIPTURE (Cycle 020, Type A PATTERN_DISCOVERY): Genesis 6 (corruption of earth, God's grief, Noah found righteous — selection criteria, provenance tracking, the 'only Noah was righteous' signal is a needle-in-haystack detection problem). Psalm 5 (morning prayer structure — systematic petition with ordered steps). John 3 (Nicodemus — the wind blows where it will, a direct stochasticity observation; born again — state transformation without visible mechanism). Daniel 2 (Nebuchadnezzar's dream — pattern recognition under uncertainty, multi-modal interpretation, the statue is structured data with multiple material layers).

DIRECTION 3 — IMPLEMENTATION SPRINT: context-lens → PyPI DAYS (prototype complete, pyproject.toml done). chain-probe → PyPI after KU-037 resolved. GitHub stars are the acquisition funnel. The window for ALL these tools is 3-6 months. Ship now. The Promptfoo acquisition at $85.5M (23 people, 23K stars) is the benchmark: reach 10K stars, get acquired.

DIRECTION 4 — PAT-064 IMPLICATION: John 2:25 ('knew what was in each person') identifies that the long-term substitution for all behavioral measurement tools is interpretability. This is both a threat (eventual obsolescence) and a bridge opportunity (build the behavioral tools now, transition to interpretability tooling in 3-5 years). The build priority is urgent BECAUSE of PAT-064 — the window is finite.

open_threads: KU-044: Optimal embedding model for semantic-pass-k — all-MiniLM-L6-v2 vs BAAI/bge-large-en vs domain-specific. Calibration study needed.
KU-045: Temperature effect on ConsistencyScore — T=0 eliminates sampling noise but not floating-point non-associativity. Baseline variance floor needed.
KU-046: Calibration of criticality tier thresholds — 0.99/0.90/0.75/0.60 are initial estimates. Empirical validation across model families needed.
KU-047: Minimum viable k — token cost is real; k=10 = 10x API cost. Calibration study for minimum k per criticality tier needed.
KU-040: cot-fidelity faithfulness_threshold calibration — default 0.92 cosine similarity needs empirical validation
KU-041: cot-fidelity extended thinking extraction — different APIs for Claude vs GPT-o3 vs Gemini
KU-042: cot-fidelity base rate — what % of real production CoT is unfaithful?
KU-043: cot-fidelity non-reasoning models — does counterfactual suppression work on standard CoT?
KU-037: fault_score threshold calibration for chain-probe v0.1 — BLOCKING for CI gate claim
KU-036: embedding model domain specificity for chain-probe EmbeddingJudge
KU-033: minimum viable position count for context-lens statistical validity
KU-009: semantic validation algorithm for llm-contract
KU-013: large diff handling for drift-guard

notes: semantic-pass-k addresses the problem that arXiv 2602.16666 formally documented and left unsolved: consistency is one of the four key dimensions of agent reliability, and it cannot be claimed from architecture — it must be measured empirically by running the same task multiple times and comparing outputs. The Numbers 23:19 pattern is not a metaphor — it is a structural observation. 'Does he speak and then not act? Does he promise and then not fulfill?' is a behavioral test protocol: run the declaration, observe the action, compare. The counterfactual consistency test flows directly from this structure. The closest competitor (AgentAssay) answers a different question. The window is 3-6 months. PAT-064 (John 2:25) identifies the long-term threat: Anthropic's interpretability research, if successful, makes behavioral testing unnecessary. This adds urgency — build now, while the window is open. [WEB-FRESH 2026-03-31] 11 searches run. GREEN status confirmed. Promptfoo feature request #5947 is the market demand signal. Fortune March 2026 confirms reliability as #1 pain point. Build now.
